summary: V7-B (drop 'blocked-' prefix) eliminated the 400 error from V7-A. Thread URL slug is now plain UUID (d2fbcc7c-dd1f-4108-9fe7-2217c88b6581). However, GET /rest/thread/<UUID> now returns HTTP 403 with error_code='VIEW_THREAD_NOT_ALLOW' and message='You do not have permission'. Backend recognizes the UUID but rejects it because anonymous user is not the thread owner. A new modal '이 스레드는 비공개입니다 (This thread is private)' appears prompting Google/Apple/email login. No warning bubble visible. The block thread is owner-locked.

network_observations.second_attempt_note: duplicate fetch was canceled (likely by route change after modal)
network_observations.comparison_to_v7a: V7-A returned HTTP 400 (URL parse error from 'blocked-{UUID}' format). V7-B with plain UUID passes URL parsing but fails at authorization layer with HTTP 403.

dom_scan.modal_subtitle_korean: 이 스레드의 소유자이거나 액세스를 요청하려면 로그인하세요
dom_scan.post_modal_dismiss_state: redirected to perplexity.ai home (URL ?), no answer page, no warning bubble

comparison_vs_697.697_v7a_outcome: ownership/permission fields fixed → modal dismissed BUT GET /rest/thread/blocked-{UUID} returned 400 (URL parse failure)
comparison_vs_697.701_v7b_outcome: blocked- prefix dropped → 400 resolved (URL parse OK), but new failure: 403 VIEW_THREAD_NOT_ALLOW (authorization layer rejects anon user)
comparison_vs_697.progress: V7-B advanced past URL parsing but blocked at authorization. The thread is created server-side but not accessible to anonymous viewer.
comparison_vs_697.regression: New private-thread login modal replaced the previous ownership modal — different blocker, same end state (no warning bubble)
comparison_vs_697.remaining_gap: Thread must be created with public/anon-readable visibility OR the warning must be delivered without requiring a thread fetch (e.g., inline replacement, alternative endpoint, or skip thread persist for blocked queries)

next_step_recommendation: Escalate to V7-C: explore options — (a) set thread visibility to public/anon-readable on creation, (b) inject warning at /perplexity_ask SSE response level (not via thread route), (c) bypass thread fetch entirely for blocked queries by returning warning content inline as if thread fetched 200 with synthetic answer payload, or (d) create thread with a known anon-accessible owner_uuid
